skill.name: dropbox-automation
skill.description: Automate Dropbox search, file transfer, folder management, sharing, and metadata workflows through the Rube MCP Dropbox toolkit.
skill.summary: Guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Dropbox automation with Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick command detections, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but the reviewed file is Markdown guidance and those detections are false positives. The real risk is legitimate but sensitive network-backed Dropbox automation through Rube MCP, including OAuth connection setup, file reads, uploads, shared links, moves, and deletes.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Dropbox Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to connect Rube MCP and authorize Dropbox access. This is expected for the skill, but it routes cloud file operations through an external MCP service and can expose Dropbox metadata or content to that integration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires Rube MCP, Dropbox OAuth connection setup, and an external MCP endpoint. This confirms a network and authorization risk, but the behavior matches the stated purpose rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Dropbox Mutating Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows for upload, move, copy, delete, batch delete, and shared link creation. These are legitimate Dropbox tasks, but mistakes or overbroad user prompts could modify or expose cloud files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences include direct file transfer, sharing, and destructive operations. The risk depends on user authorization and confirmation, so medium severity is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command detections are Markdown inline code spans for MCP tool names, parameters, and Dropbox values. No executable Ruby code, shell invocation, or command interpolation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown instructions and quick-reference entries, not source code. The repeated detections are caused by backtick formatting around tool names and parameters.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts map to descriptive text and Dropbox field names, not cryptographic APIs or local system inspection. No evidence found of hashing, cipher use, host enumeration, or local system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe Dropbox automation, pagination, shared links, and path fields. They do not contain executable code or system-level behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Endpoint Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to add an external Rube MCP endpoint before using Dropbox tools. Users should understand that Dropbox automation depends on that third-party service connection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented. The pattern is not malicious, but it is a real external service dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Cloud File Deletion and Sharing Workflows
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes delete, batch delete, move, copy, and shared link workflows. These operations can change access or remove Dropbox content when executed with an authorized connection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines list the exact Dropbox operations and parameters. The risk is capability-based and requires user authorization.

content.value_statement: Dropbox file work can require many manual searches, transfers, and permission checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for safe Dropbox automation through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Search Dropbox files and folders by query, path, category, extension, and filename match.
Read, upload, export, download, and save files through Dropbox MCP tools.
Create and manage shared links with audience, access, expiration, password, and download settings.
Create, move, copy, rename, and delete Dropbox files and folders, including batch workflows.
List folder contents, handle pagination, and fetch item metadata before later operations.
Guide users through Rube MCP connection setup and Dropbox OAuth activation checks.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Dropbox connection before any Dropbox workflow can run.
Does not include executable helper scripts or local Dropbox API client code.
Dropbox API limits, pagination, batch job delays, and file size limits still apply.
Destructive actions require careful user confirmation because the skill can guide delete and overwrite workflows.

use_cases.title: Find Project Files Quickly
use_cases.description: Search a Dropbox workspace, verify matches with metadata, and continue pagination until all relevant files are found.

use_cases.title: Prepare Secure Shared Links
use_cases.description: Validate a file path, check for existing links, and create a controlled shared link with the requested access settings.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Folder Structures
use_cases.description: Create folders, move files, rename items, and batch delete confirmed obsolete content using Dropbox tool sequences.

prompt_templates.title: Connect Dropbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Dropbox automation skill to check whether Rube MCP is available and help me connect my Dropbox account.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Dropbox for documents matching “quarterly report” under /Finance. Continue pagination and summarize the strongest matches.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Protected Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a viewer-only shared link for /Client/Proposal.pdf that expires at the end of the month. Check for an existing link first.

prompt_templates.title: Reorganize a Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a safe batch move from /Incoming to /Archive/2026. Validate paths, identify conflicts, and ask before executing any delete or overwrite action.

output_examples.input: Find all PDF invoices in /Accounting for April.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches the requested folder, follows any result cursor, verifies matching metadata, and returns a concise list of invoice file names and Dropbox paths.

output_examples.input: Share /Projects/Roadmap.pdf with a client as view-only.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the file exists, checks for an existing direct link, then prepares a viewer link with the requested audience and expiration settings.
The assistant lists candidate files, validates destination paths, explains conflicts, and waits for approval before running the move workflow.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first so the current Dropbox tool schemas are used.
Validate every Dropbox path with metadata before sharing, moving, deleting, or overwriting files.
Ask for explicit confirmation before destructive operations or broad batch changes.
anti_patterns: Do not create public shared links without confirming audience, expiration, and download settings.
Do not stop after the first search page when Dropbox reports more results are available.
Do not use user-typed paths for write actions when API metadata returns a canonical path.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Dropbox API key?
faq.answer: No. It expects Rube MCP and a Dropbox OAuth connection managed through the MCP tools.

faq.question: Can it upload and download files?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Dropbox upload, read, export, zip download, shared link download, and save URL workflows.

faq.question: Can it delete files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes delete and batch delete workflows, so users should require clear confirmation first.

faq.question: Does it run local shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance for MCP tools and does not contain executable shell or Ruby code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: It uses an external MCP service with authorized Dropbox access and can guide file sharing, upload, move, and delete actions.

faq.question: What should users check before sharing files?
faq.answer: Users should verify the canonical Dropbox path, reuse existing links when appropriate, and set audience, access, expiration, and password controls.
